records.ety: [OE. dawnen, dawen, dagen, daien, AS. dagian to become day, to dawn, fr. dæg day; akin to D. dagen, G. tagen, Icel. daga, Dan. dages, Sw. dagas. See Day. √71.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dawned (dnd); p. pr. & vb. n. Dawning.]

defs: To begin to grow light in the morning; to grow light; to break, or begin to appear; as, the day dawns; the morning dawns.
quotes: In the end of the Sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene . . . to see the sepulcher.

defs: To began to give promise; to begin to appear or to expand.
quotes: When life awakes, and dawns at every line.
Dawn on our darkness and lend us thine aid.

defs: The break of day; the first appearance of light in the morning; show of approaching sunrise.
quotes: And oft at dawn, deep noon, or falling eve.
No sun, no moon, no morn, no noon, No dawn, no dusk, no proper time of day.

defs: First opening or expansion; first appearance; beginning; rise.
quotes: These tender circumstances diffuse a dawn of serenity over the soul.
